title: Protests Erupt In Morón As Cubans Attack Communist Party Office | 10 News
description: The Cuban government is threatening a security crackdown after protesters attacked and burned a communist party building in the central city of Moron.  Moments later gunfire broke out, and several people were reportedly shot Cuban state media later denied that announcing five arrests.  Cuba is struggling with a growing energy crisis after America cut off its only supply line of fuel from Venezuela food and medicine are also facing dire shortages. title: Doomsday Prepper Shares His Survival, From Bunkers To Armoured Cars | 10 News+
description: Trevor Andrei is a doomsday prepper who has an armoured vehicle and a well‑stocked underground bunker.   He believes that the ongoing fuel crisis - a knock‑on effect of the war in Iran - could be the next major disaster to hit Australia, similar to the toilet paper shortages seen during the COVID‑19 pandemic.  10 News+ sat down with Trevor at a secret location to discuss whether he’s paranoid or simply prepared. title: Benjamin Netanyahu Dismisses Iran Death Rumours | 10 News
description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has posted a video of himself getting a cup of coffee on Sunday after rumours that he was dead circulated online in Iran.  The video, taken at a café in Jerusalem, shows Netanyahu talking to his aide asking about the rumours.  Netanyahu was quick to respond with a ​pun on the word dead, which in Hebrew slang ⁠can be used to describe "being crazy about" something, as ​he reaches for a cup of coffee  Join the conversation and follow us on: X: https://x.com/10NewsAU Facebook: http://www.facebook.com/10NewsAU/ Instagram: http://www.instagram.com/10NewsAU/ TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@10NewsAU LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/10newsau  10 News airs at 5pm, seven nights a week in your local market on Network 10. title: Pope Leo XIV Calls For Immediate Ceasefire In Middle East | 10 News
description: Pope Leo XIV has called for an immediate ceasefire in the Middle East, two weeks since the US and Israel launched their first attacks on Iran.  The pontiff added during his traditional Angelus prayer in St Peter's Square that civilians in the Middle East “have been suffering the atrocious violence of war” for weeks.  "In the name of Christians in the Middle East and of all women and men of good will, I appeal to those responsible for this conflict: Ceasefire!" the pontiff said.
